steps.title: Fit the standard-form line
steps.content: Instead of rearranging the equation, use custom regression with the exact form from the stem. The line $ax+ky=6$ contains $(-2,-6)$ and $(0,-3)$. Put those two points in Desmos and fit $a x_1+k y_1\sim6$.

steps.title: Read $k$ directly
steps.content: The regression returns $k=-2$. It also returns $a=3$, so the line is $3x-2y=6$.

steps.title: Confirm with the easy point
steps.content: The point $(0,-3)$ makes the original equation especially quick: $a(0)+k(-3)=6$, so $-3k=6$ and $k=-2$. Choice A is correct.
